title: Nested Loops
description: Place loops inside of other loops for complex tasks.

slides.title: Loops Inside Loops 🪆
slides.content:
It is also possible to place a loop inside another loop. This is called a **nested loop**.

The "inner loop" will be executed one time for *each iteration* of the "outer loop".

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Let's see an outer loop and an inner loop.

quizData.question: In the code above, the outer loop runs 2 times. The inner loop condition runs 3 times sequentially. In total, how many times does 'Inner' get printed?
quizData.explanation: For each of the 2 outer loop iterations, the inner loop runs 3 times. 2 * 3 = 6.

slides.title: Use Cases 🎮
slides.content:
Nested loops are incredibly common when dealing with 2D structures (grids, chessboards, spreadsheets).

You use the outer loop for the rows, and the inner loop for the columns.

quizData.question: An inner loop completely finishes all of its cycles before the outer loop continues to its next iteration.
quizData.explanation: Yes. The inner loop must complete its full set of iterations before the outer loop can move on.
